lines: @@ -11,5 +11,4 @@ DuckDB-specific integration files in this directory are:
- `aoc24.csv` for manually running the AoC benchmark suite through the regression tooling
+The vendored benchmark suite covers all 25 days.
+# description: Run Advent of Code 2024 day16 SQL workload
+DESCRIPTION=Run Advent of Code 2024 day16 SQL workload
+# description: Run Advent of Code 2024 day17 SQL workload
+DESCRIPTION=Run Advent of Code 2024 day17 SQL workload
+# description: Run Advent of Code 2024 day22 SQL workload
+DESCRIPTION=Run Advent of Code 2024 day22 SQL workload
+# description: Run Advent of Code 2024 day24 SQL workload
+DESCRIPTION=Run Advent of Code 2024 day24 SQL workload
+   select 0, substr(i,1,position(E'\n' in i)-1), substr(i,position(E'\n' in i)+1)
+   select y+1,substr(r,1,position(E'\n' in r)-1), substr(r,position(E'\n' in r)+1)
+   from lines l(y,l,r) where position(E'\n' in r)>0
+   from (select * from lines l where line<>'') s, lateral generate_series(1,length(line)) g(x)
+walls(x,y) as (select x,y from field where v='#'),
+startpos(x,y) as (select x,y from field where v='S' limit 1),
+endpos(x,y) as (select x,y from field where v='E' limit 1),
+         select r,x,y,(d+td)%4 as d,c+tc as c from tmp cross join turns
+      from agg a natural join directions where not exists(select * from walls w where a.x+dx=w.x and a.y+dy=w.y)
+part1(x,y,d,c) as (select x,y,d,min(c) from (select x,y,(d+td)%4 as d, (c+tc) as c from part1steps cross join turns) group by x,y,d),
+best1(c) as (select min(c) from part1 natural join endpos),
+   select * from part1 natural join endpos where c=(select c from best1)
+    select p.x, p.y, p.d, p.c from tmp t cross join turns, part1 p
+    where p.x=t.x and p.y=t.y and p.d=(t.d+4-td)%4 and p.c=t.c-tc and td>0
+    select p.x, p.y, p.d, p.c from tmp t natural join directions, part1 p
+    where p.x=t.x-dx and p.y=t.y-dy and p.d=t.d and p.c=t.c-1)),
+select (select * from best1) as part1, (select count(*) from part2) as part2;
+   select 0, substr(i,1,position(E'\n' in i)-1), substr(i,position(E'\n' in i)+1)
+   select y+1,substr(r,1,position(E'\n' in r)-1), substr(r,position(E'\n' in r)+1)
+   from lines l(y,l,r) where position(E'\n' in r)>0
+splitlines(y,head, value) as (select y, substr(line, 1, position(': ' in line)-1) as head, substr(line, position(': ' in line)+2) as value from lines where line like '%: %'),
+   from splitlines a, splitlines b, splitlines c
+   where a.head = 'Register A' and b.head = 'Register B' and c.head = 'Register C'
+   select 0 as step, substr(value, 1, position(',' in value)-1)::bigint as value, substr(value, position(',' in value)+1) as tail

+   select step+1, case when tail like '%,%' then substr(tail, 1, position(',' in tail)-1)::bigint else tail::bigint end, case when tail like '%,%' then substr(tail, position(',' in tail)+1) else '' end
+   from rawprog p1 left join rawprog p2 on p1.step+1=p2.step),
+   select 0 as step,0::bigint as ip,a,b,c,'' as out from state
+          case when op=3 and a<>0 then arg else ip end,
+          case op when 0 then case when combo not between 0 and 63 then 0 else a>>combo::integer end else a end,
+          case op when 1 then xor(b, arg) when 2 then combo & 7 when 4 then xor(b, c) when 6 then case when combo not between 0 and 63 then 0 else a>>combo::integer end else b end,
+          case op when 7 then case when combo not between 0 and 63 then 0 else a>>combo::integer end else c end,
+          case op when 5 then case when out<>'' then out||','||(combo&7) else (combo&7)::text end else out end
+   from (select step, ip+2 as ip, a, b, c, out, op, arg, case arg when 0 then 0 when 1 then 1 when 2 then 2 when 3 then 3 when 4 then a when 5 then b when 6 then c else 0 end::bigint as combo
+         from simulation join program on ip=pos)
+part1(out) as (select out from simulation where step=(select max(step) from simulation)),
+   select 0::bigint as v, (select count(*) from program) as remaining
+   select 0 as step,8*v+t as v,0::bigint as ip,8*v+t as a,b,c,'' as out from state,tmp, generate_series(0,7) g(t) where remaining>0
+          case when op=3 and a<>0 then arg else ip end,
+          case op when 0 then case when combo not between 0 and 63 then 0 else a>>combo::integer end else a end,
+          case op when 1 then xor(b, arg) when 2 then combo & 7 when 4 then xor(b, c) when 6 then case when combo not between 0 and 63 then 0 else a>>combo::integer end else b end,
+          case op when 7 then case when combo not between 0 and 63 then 0 else a>>combo::integer end else c end,
+          case op when 5 then case when out<>'' then out||','||(combo&7) else (combo&7)::text end else out end
+   from (select step, seed,ip+2 as ip, a, b, c, out, op, arg, case arg when 0 then 0 when 1 then 1 when 2 then 2 when 3 then 3 when 4 then a when 5 then b when 6 then c else 0 end::bigint as combo
+         from simulation join program on ip=pos)),
+   simulationend(seed,out) as (select seed, out from simulation s1 where step=(select max(s2.step) from simulation s2 where s2.seed=s1.seed)),
+   expected(out) as (select string_agg(op::text, ',' order by pos) as out from program, rem where pos>=remaining-1)
+   select seed, remaining-1 from simulationend s, expected e, rem where remaining>0 and s.out=e.out
+       (select min(v) from part2 where remaining=0) as part2
+   select 0, substr(i,1,position(E'\n' in i)-1), substr(i,position(E'\n' in i)+1)
+   select y+1,substr(r,1,position(E'\n' in r)-1), substr(r,position(E'\n' in r)+1)
+   from lines l(y,l,r) where position(E'\n' in r)>0
+secrets(y,v) as (select y, line::bigint from lines where line<>''),
+      select y, step, xor(n // 32, n) % 16777216 as n from (
+      select y, step, xor(v * 64, v) % 16777216 as n
+      from sequences where step<2000) s) s),
+   select y, step, v%10 as v0, lead(v%10, 1) over w as v1, lead(v%10, 2) over w as v2, lead(v%10, 3) over w as v3, lead(v%10, 4) over w as v4
+   from sequences where step<2000-4 window w as (partition by y order by step)) s
+gains(y,enc,v) as (select y, enc, v from rawgains g1 where not exists (select * from rawgains g2 where g1.y=g2.y and g1.enc=g2.enc and g2.step<g1.step)),
+gainsperenc(enc,v) as (select enc, sum(v) from gains group by enc)
+select (select sum(v) from sequences where step = 2000) as part1,
+   select 0, substr(i,1,position(E'\n' in i)-1), substr(i,position(E'\n' in i)+1)
+   select y+1,substr(r,1,position(E'\n' in r)-1), substr(r,position(E'\n' in r)+1)
+   from lines l(y,l,r) where position(E'\n' in r)>0
+   select substr(line, 1, position(': ' in line)-1) as r, substr(line, position(': ' in line)+2)::bigint as value from lines where line like '%: %'),
+   select ll, substr(lr, 1, position(' ' in lr)-1), substr(lr, position(' ' in lr)+1), sr from (
+   select substr(sl, 1, position(' ' in sl)-1) as ll, substr(sl, position(' ' in sl)+1) as lr, sr from (
+   select substr(line, 1, position(' -> ' in line)-1) as sl, substr(line, position(' -> ' in line)+4) as sr from lines where line like '% -> %') s) s
+    select r1.step, g.out, case g.op when 'AND' then r1.v & r2.v when 'OR' then r1.v | r2.v when 'XOR' then xor(r1.v, r2.v) end
+    from gates g, tmp r1, tmp r2
+    select * from tmp t where t.r not in (select r from prop))
+    where exists(select * from next n left join tmp t on n.r=t.r where t.v is distinct from n.v))),
+part1values(r,v) as (select r,v from part1raw where step=(select max(step) from part1raw)),
+part1(v) as (select sum(case when r like 'z%' then v<<substr(r,2)::integer else 0 end) as v from part1values where r like 'z%'),
+   select 0, 1, g.out, '' from gates g where g.op = 'AND' and ((g.in1='x00' and g.in2='y00') or (g.in2='x00' and g.in1='y00'))

+      state(step, digit, x, y, z, last_carry) as (select step, digit, case when digit < 10 then 'x0'||digit else 'x'||digit end, case when digit < 10 then 'y0'||digit else 'y'||digit end, case when digit < 10 then 'z0'||digit else 'z'||digit end, v1 from tmp where v2='' limit 1),
+      swaps(v1,v2) as (select v1, v2 from tmp where v2<>''),
+      swaps2(v1,v2) as (select v1, v2 from swaps union all select v2, v1 from swaps),
+      swappedgates(in1,op,in2,out) as (select in1, op, in2, coalesce(s.v2, g.out) from gates g left join swaps2 s on g.out=s.v1),
+      and_gate(ag) as (select out from swappedgates g, state s where g.op='AND' and ((g.in1=s.x and g.in2=s.y) or (g.in2=s.x and g.in1=s.y))),
+      xor_gate(xg) as (select out from swappedgates g, state s where g.op='XOR' and ((g.in1=s.x and g.in2=s.y) or (g.in2=s.x and g.in1=s.y))),
+      setz_gate(r1,r2) as (select in1, in2 from swappedgates g, state s where g.out=s.z),
+      swapforsetz(s1,s2) as (select r2, xg from setz_gate, xor_gate, state where r1=last_carry and r2<>xg union all select r1, xg from setz_gate, xor_gate, state where r2=last_carry and r1<>xg),
+      z_gate(zg) as (select out from swappedgates g, state s, xor_gate where g.op='XOR' and ((g.in1=xg and g.in2=last_carry) or (g.in2=xg and g.in1=last_carry))),
+      swapforz(s1,s2) as (select zg, z from z_gate, state where zg<>z),
+      tg(tg) as (select out from swappedgates g, xor_gate, state where g.op='AND' and ((g.in1=xg and g.in2=last_carry) or (g.in2=xg and g.in1=last_carry))),
+      next_carry(ng) as (select out from swappedgates g, tg, and_gate where g.op='OR' and ((g.in1=tg and g.in2=ag) or (g.in2=tg and g.in1=ag))),
+      scenario as (select case when exists (select * from swapforsetz) then 0 when exists (select * from swapforz) then 1 else 2 end as sc),
+      select step+1, digit+case when sc=2 then 1 else 0 end, case when sc=2 then ng else last_carry end, '' from state left join next_carry on true, scenario
+      select step+1, digit+case when sc=2 then 1 else 0 end, v1, v2 from swaps, state, scenario
+      select step+1, digit, s1, s2 from swapforsetz, state, scenario where sc=0
+      select step+1, digit, s1, s2 from swapforz, state, scenario where sc=1)
+      select step, digit, v1, v2 from next where digit<(select count(*) from assignments where r like 'x%')
+   select v1 from part2 where v2<>'' and step = (select max(step) from part2)
+   select v2 from part2 where v2<>'' and step = (select max(step) from part2)
+       (select string_agg(s,',' order by s) from swappedpart2) as part2
+	//! Whether this predicate originated from a LogicalFilter rather than a join condition.
+	//! Logical filters above a LEFT join can depend on NULL-extended rows and must remain filters.
//! Index of the equivalence group for INNER equality/IS NOT DISTINCT FROM join filters.
//! All filters transitively connected by equality (a=b, b=c -> a=c all share the same index).
//! Used by cardinality estimation to skip redundant transitive conditions.
// now check if it can be used as a join predicate
// For LEFT joins, create one FilterInfo per comparison condition.
// Each uses the full left/right relation sets from the comparison conditions
// to preserve join-ordering constraints, but individual column bindings so that
// after the LEFT join has introduced NULL-extended rows. Pin them to the full LEFT
// join output, otherwise reconstruction can push null-aware predicates like
// "lhs IS DISTINCT FROM rhs" below the LEFT join and turn them into inner filters.
@@ -254,6 +254,35 @@ SELECT * FROM t1 LEFT JOIN t2 ON a=b WHERE a IN (SELECT c FROM t3) AND b IS NOT
+# A post-LEFT SEMI join must not be reconstructed as a filter, which would preserve RHS duplicates.
+LEFT JOIN semi_r r ON l.id = r.id
+WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM semi_s s WHERE s.id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM r.id)
# LOJ followed by anti join via NOT IN (no NULLs in subquery)
@@ -274,6 +303,45 @@ SELECT * FROM t1 LEFT JOIN t2 ON a=b WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t3 WHERE a=
+# A post-LEFT ANTI join must retain its join type during reconstruction.
+LEFT JOIN semi_r r ON l.id = r.id
+WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM anti_s s WHERE s.id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM r.id)
+# An actual INNER join above a LEFT join must remain a join rather than a cross product plus filter.
+FROM (semi_l l LEFT JOIN semi_r r ON l.id = r.id)
+JOIN inner_s s ON coalesce(r.id, l.id) = s.id
+FROM (semi_l l LEFT JOIN semi_r r ON l.id = r.id)
+JOIN inner_s s ON coalesce(r.id, l.id) = s.id;
# Inner join + semi join + LOJ combination
+# A post-LEFT filter can naturally reference exactly the same two relations as the join.
+# Its relation set must still act as an ordering barrier rather than becoming an ON condition.
+left join lj_pin_r r on l.id = r.id
+# Check the inverse NULL-aware comparison and the opposite expression orientation.
+left join lj_pin_r r on l.id = r.id
+where l.v is not distinct from r.v
+# A NULL-aware comparison that references both sides must also remain above the LEFT join.
+left join lj_indistinct_r r on l.id = r.id
+where l.v is not distinct from r.v;
+# A higher LEFT join condition must stay above the nullable side of a lower LEFT join.
+left join lj_nested_b b on a.id = b.id
+left join lj_nested_c c on b.id is not distinct from c.id
+# A post-LEFT filter in an EXISTS controls convergence of the recursion.
+			SELECT step, 'z', v FROM tmp WHERE r = 'x'
+			LEFT JOIN tmp p ON n.r = p.r
+			WHERE p.v IS DISTINCT FROM n.v
+		next(step, r, v) AS NOT MATERIALIZED (
+			SELECT step, 'z', v FROM tmp WHERE r = 'x'
+			LEFT JOIN tmp p ON n.r = p.r
+			WHERE p.v IS DISTINCT FROM n.v
